questions.question: Which of the following is a time complexity of Bubble Sort?
questions.explanation: Bubble sort is a simple sorting algorithm that repeatedly steps through the list, compares adjacent elements, and swaps them if they are in the wrong order. This algorithm is less efficient than other sorting algorithms like quicksort or mergesort, with a worst-case time complexity of O(n^2).

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of Insertion Sort?
questions.explanation: Insertion sort is an in-place comparison-based sorting algorithm that works by dividing the input into a sorted and an unsorted region. Each subsequent element from the unsorted region is inserted into the sorted region in its correct position.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of Selection Sort?
questions.explanation: Selection sort is an in-place comparison-based sorting algorithm. It works by dividing the input into two parts: the sorted region at the left end and the unsorted region at the right end. Elements from the unsorted region are selected and inserted into the sorted region in their correct position.

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of Bubble Sort?
questions.explanation: Bubble sort is not an adaptable sorting algorithm because it always requires a number of swaps proportional to the number of elements being sorted. It is also not an in-place sorting algorithm because it requires additional space for temporary storage. Bubble sort is also not stable, meaning equal elements may not keep their original order.

questions.question: Which of the following is a comparison between the number of operations performed by Bubble Sort and Insertion Sort?
options: Bubble Sort (n) vs Insertion Sort (n log n)
Bubble Sort (n log n) vs Insertion Sort (n^2)
questions.explanation: Bubble sort has a time complexity of O(n^2), whereas insertion sort has an average time complexity of O(n log n). This makes insertion sort more efficient than bubble sort for large datasets.
